name: Command Line Execution with Suspicious URL and AppData Strings
notes:
This rule detects a suspicious command line execution that includes an URL and AppData string in the command line parameters as used by several droppers.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
SIGMA Title: Command Line Execution with Suspicious URL and AppData Strings
SIGMA Description: Detects a suspicious command line execution that includes an URL and AppData string in the command line parameters as used by several droppers (js/vbs > powershell)


Confidence: HIGH

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success
when the event matches Command (custom) contains all of [cmd or http or :// or AppData]
TD_list: APPLY Command Line Execution with Suspicious URL and AppData Strings on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success
AND when the event matches Command (custom) contains all of [cmd or http or :// or AppData]
RED_list: Event name: Command Line Execution with Suspicious URL and AppData Strings
Event description: Detects a suspicious command line execution that includes an URL and AppData string in the command line parameters as used by several droppers
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
